subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Region R: y=2x and y=x² — Area, sin(πx/2) Cross-Section Volume & Square Cross-Section (Write Only)
stimulus: Let $R$ be the region in the first quadrant enclosed by the graphs of $y = 2x$ and $y = x^2$, as shown in the figure.

parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Area $= \int_0^2(2x - x^2)\, dx = \left[x^2 - \frac{x^3}{3}\right]_0^2 = 4 - \frac{8}{3} = \frac{4}{3}$.

parts.prompt: The region $R$ is the base of a solid. For this solid, at each $x$ the cross section perpendicular to the $x$-axis has area $A(x) = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}x\right)$. Find the volume of the solid.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Volume $= \int_0^2 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}x\right)\, dx = \left[-\frac{2}{\pi}\cos\left(\frac{\pi x}{2}\right)\right]_0^2 = \frac{4}{\pi}$.

parts.prompt: Another solid has the same base $R$. For this solid, the cross sections perpendicular to the $y$-axis are squares. Write, but do not evaluate, an integral expression for the volume of the solid.
rubric_criteria: 2 pts: Correct integrand: express boundaries in terms of $y$: $y = 2x \Rightarrow x = y/2$; $y = x^2 \Rightarrow x = \sqrt{y}$; side of square $= \sqrt{y} - y/2$; area $= \left(\sqrt{y} - \frac{y}{2}\right)^2$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Volume $= \int_0^4 \left(\sqrt{y} - \frac{y}{2}\right)^2\, dy$ (do not evaluate).
